volumeTitle: Trojan Women. Iphigenia among the Taurians. Ion
label: Trojan Women (Section 565 - Section 3.657)

sections.label: Section 565
sections.translation: a glorious crown for Hellas won , for her , the nurse of youth , but for our Phrygian fatherland a bitter grief .

sections.label: Section 568
sections.translation: Hecuba , do you see Andromache advancing here on a foreign chariot ?

sections.label: Section 570
sections.translation: and with her , clasped to her throbbing breast , is her dear Astyanax , Hector ’ s child . Where are you being carried , unhappy wife , mounted on that chariot , side by side with Hector ’ s brazen arms and Phrygian spoils of war ,

sections.label: Section 575
sections.translation: with which Achilles ’ son will deck the shrines of Phthia on his return from Troy ?

sections.label: Section 1.577
sections.translation: My Achaean masters are leading me away .

sections.label: Section 1.578
sections.translation: Ah me !

sections.label: Section 1.578b
sections.translation: Why do you in note of woe utter the dirge that is mine ?

sections.label: Section 1.579
sections.translation: Alas —

sections.label: Section 1.579b
sections.translation: For these sorrows —

sections.label: Section 1.580
sections.translation: O Zeus —

sections.label: Section 1.580b
sections.translation: And for this calamity .

sections.label: Section 1.581
sections.translation: O my children !

sections.label: Section 1.581b
sections.translation: Our day is past .

sections.label: Section 1.582
sections.translation: Joy is gone , Troy is gone .

sections.label: Section 1.583
sections.translation: Unhappy !

sections.label: Section 1.583b
sections.translation: For my gallant sons

sections.label: Section 1.584
sections.translation: Alas !

sections.label: Section 1.584b
sections.translation: Alas indeed , for my

sections.label: Section 1.585
sections.translation: Misery !

sections.label: Section 1.585b
sections.translation: Piteous the fate

sections.label: Section 1.586
sections.translation: Of our city ,

sections.label: Section 1.586b
sections.translation: Smouldering in the smoke .

sections.label: Section 2.587
sections.translation: Come to me , my husband , come to me !

sections.label: Section 2.588
sections.translation: Ah , hapless wife ! you call on my son who lies in the tomb .

sections.label: Section 2.590
sections.translation: Your wife ’ s defender !

sections.label: Section 2.591
sections.translation: Oh , you , who before made the Achaeans grieve , eldest of the sons I bore to Priam , take me to your rest in Hades ’ halls !

sections.label: Section 3.595
sections.translation: These great griefs —

sections.label: Section 3.595b
sections.translation: Unhappy one , bitter these woes to bear .

sections.label: Section 3.596
sections.translation: Our city ruined —

sections.label: Section 3.596b
sections.translation: And sorrow to sorrow added .

sections.label: Section 3.597
sections.original: δυσφροσύναισι θεῶν , ὅτε σὸς γόνος ἔκφυγεν Ἅιδαν ,
sections.translation: Through the will of angry heaven , since the day that son [*] of yours escaped death , he that for a hated bride brought destruction on the Trojan citadel . There lie the gory corpses of the slain by the shrine of Pallas for vultures to carry off ;

sections.label: Section 3.600
sections.original: γυψὶ φέρειν τέταται · ζυγὰ δʼ ἤνυσε
sections.translation: and Troy has come to slavery ’ s yoke .

sections.label: Section 3.601
sections.translation: O my country , O unhappy land —

sections.label: Section 3.601b
sections.translation: I weep for you now left behind .

sections.label: Section 3.602
sections.translation: Now do you behold your piteous end .

sections.label: Section 3.602b
sections.translation: And you , my house , where I gave birth .

sections.label: Section 3.603
sections.original: ὦ τέκνʼ , ἐρημόπολις μάτηρ ἀπολείπεται ὑμῶν ,
sections.translation: O my children ! bereft of her city as your mother is , she now is losing you . Oh , what mourning and what sorrow ! . . .

sections.label: Section 3.605
sections.translation: oh , what endless streams of tears in our houses ! The dead alone forget their griefs and never shed a tear .

sections.label: Section 3.608
sections.original: ὡς ἡδὺ δάκρυα τοῖς κακῶς πεπραγόσι
sections.translation: What sweet relief to sufferers it is to weep , to mourn , lament , and chant the dirge that tells of grief !

sections.label: Section 3.610
sections.original: ὦ μῆτερ ἀνδρός , ὅς ποτʼ Ἀργείων δορὶ
sections.translation: Do you see this , mother of that man , Hector , who once laid low in battle many a son of Argos ?

sections.label: Section 3.612
sections.original: ὁρῶ τὰ τῶν θεῶν , ὡς τὰ μὲν πυργοῦσʼ ἄνω
sections.translation: I see that it is heaven ’ s way to exalt what men accounted nothing , and ruin what they most esteemed .

sections.label: Section 3.614
sections.original: ἀγόμεθα λεία σὺν τέκνῳ · τὸ δʼ εὐγενὲς
sections.translation: Hence with my child as booty am I borne ; the noble

sections.label: Section 3.615
sections.original: ἐς δοῦλον ἥκει , μεταβολὰς τοσάσδʼ ἔχον .
sections.translation: are brought to slavery — a bitter change .

sections.label: Section 3.616
sections.original: τὸ τῆς ἀνάγκης δεινόν · ἄρτι κἀπʼ ἐμοῦ
sections.translation: This is necessity ’ s grim law ; it was just now that Cassandra was torn with brutal violence from my arms .

sections.label: Section 3.618
sections.translation: Alas , alas ! it seems a second Aias has appeared to wrong your daughter ; but there are other ills for you .

sections.label: Section 3.620
sections.original: ὧν γʼ οὔτε μέτρον οὔτʼ ἀριθμός ἐστί μοι ·
sections.translation: Yes , beyond all count or measure are my sorrows ; evil vies with evil in the struggle to be first .

sections.label: Section 3.622
sections.original: τέθνηκέ σοι παῖς πρὸς τάφῳ Πολυξένη
sections.translation: Your daughter Polyxena is dead , slain at Achilles ’ tomb , an offering to his lifeless corpse .

sections.label: Section 3.624
sections.original: οἲ ʼγὼ τάλαινα . τοῦτʼ ἐκεῖνʼ ὅ μοι πάλαι
sections.translation: O woe is me ! This is that riddle Talthybius

sections.label: Section 3.625
sections.original: Ταλθύβιος αἴνιγμʼ οὐ σαφῶς εἶπεν σαφές .
sections.translation: long ago told me , a truth obscurely uttered .

sections.label: Section 3.626
sections.original: εἶδόν νιν αὐτή , κἀποβᾶσα τῶνδʼ ὄχων
sections.translation: I saw her myself ; so I alighted from the chariot , and covered her corpse with a mantle , and struck upon my breast .

sections.label: Section 3.628
sections.translation: Alas ! my child , for your unhallowed sacrifice ! and yet again , alas ! for your shameful death !

sections.label: Section 3.630
sections.original: ὄλωλεν ὡς ὄλωλεν · ἀλλʼ ὅμως ἐμοῦ
sections.translation: Her death was even as it was , and yet that death of hers was after all a happier fate than my life .

sections.label: Section 3.632
sections.original: οὐ ταὐτόν , ὦ παῖ , τῷ βλέπειν τὸ κατθανεῖν ·
sections.translation: Death and life are not the same , my child ; the one is annihilation , the other keeps a place for hope .

sections.label: Section 3.634
sections.original: ὦ μῆτερ , ὦ τεκοῦσα , κάλλιστον λόγον
sections.translation: Hear , O mother of children ! give ear to what I urge so well ,

sections.label: Section 3.635
sections.original: ἄκουσον , ὥς σοι τέρψιν ἐμβαλῶ φρενί .
sections.translation: that I may cheer my drooping spirit . It is all one , I say , never to have been born and to be dead , and better far is death than life with misery . For the dead feel no sorrow any more and know no grief ; but he who has known prosperity and has fallen on evil days

sections.label: Section 3.640
sections.translation: feels his spirit straying from the scene of former joys . Now that child of yours is dead as though she never had seen the light , and little she knows of her calamity ; whereas I , who aimed at a fair repute , though I won a higher lot than most , yet missed my luck in life .

sections.label: Section 3.645
sections.original: ἃ γὰρ γυναιξὶ σώφρονʼ ἔσθʼ ηὑρημένα ,
sections.translation: For all that stamps the wife a woman chaste , I strove to do in Hector ’ s home . In the first place , whether there is a slur upon a woman , or whether there is not , the very fact of her not staying at home brings in its train an evil name ;

sections.label: Section 3.650
sections.original: τούτου παρεῖσα πόθον ἔμιμνον ἐν δόμοις ·
sections.translation: therefore I gave up any longing to do so , and stayed within my house ; nor would I admit indoors the clever gossip women love , but conscious of a heart that told an honest tale I was content . And ever would I keep a silent tongue and modest eye before my husband ;

sections.label: Section 3.655
sections.original: παρεῖχον · ᾔδη δʼ ἁμὲ χρῆν νικᾶν πόσιν ,
sections.translation: and well I knew where I might rule him , and where it was best to yield .

sections.label: Section 3.657
sections.original: καὶ τῶνδε κληδὼν ἐς στράτευμʼ Ἀχαιϊκὸν
sections.translation: Report of this has reached the Achaean army , and proved my ruin ; for when I was taken captive , Achilles ’ son would have me
